# Team News: Austria vs. Jordan injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Ellis Stevens| 16 Jun 2026 05:00, Last updated: 16 Jun 2026 05:00

© Imago / STEINSIEK.CH

Austria and Jordan get their respective 2026 World Cup campaigns underway when they meet in a Group J clash on Wednesday morning.

Austria return to the World Cup stage for the first time since 1998, while Jordan will be making their first-ever appearance at the competition.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up all of the latest team news for both nations.

---

## AUSTRIA vs. JORDAN

## AUSTRIA

Out: None

Doubtful: David Alaba(unspecified), Florian Grillitsch(unspecified), Patrick Wimmer(unspecified)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: A Schlager; Laimer, Lienhart, Alaba, Mwene; X Schalger, Seiwald; Schmid, Sabitzer, Gregoritsch; Arnautovic

## JORDAN

Out: None

Doubtful: None

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Abulaila; Obaid, Al-Arab, Nasib; Haddad, Al Rashdan, Al Rawabdeh, Taha; Tamari, Olwan, Fakhouri

---

Written by

Share this article:

news_items.title: Austria vs Jordan team news and predicted lineups | Squawka | EN
news_items.text:
Austria vs Jordan team news and predicted lineups | Squawka | EN

Squawka/ News/ World Cup/ Austria vs Jordan team news and predicted lineups

# Austria vs Jordan team news and predicted lineups

- Harry Edwards
- 16 hours ago

- Copied

Austria get their World Cup 2026 campaign underway when they face Jordan on Wednesday at 5am (UK time).

Austria are back at the World Cup after a 28-year absence, since their group-stage exit in 1998. It was their longest absence from the tournament, beating the 20 years they missed out between 1958 and 1978. Austria had early success at the World Cup with strong finishes in 1934 and 1954, but managed just one win across 1990 and 1998.

Jordan, meanwhile, are one of the World Cup debutants at this summer’s tournament. They have been placed in a tough group but will be history makers whatever happens. And they’ll have already seen Curacao score in their very first World Cup.

This will be the first meeting between these two sides.

## Austria vs Jordan team news

### Austria team news

Ralf Rangnick suffered a blow in the buildup to the World Cup as Christoph Baumgartner suffered an injury, ruling him out of the tournament. Baumgartner had been fav

news_items.title: Austria vs Jordan at World Cup 2026: TV channel, how to watch, kick-off time, live stream, referee, predicted line-ups - ESPN
news_items.author: Sunaadh Sagar
news_items.text:
Austria vs Jordan at World Cup 2026: TV channel, how to watch, kick-off time, live stream, referee, predicted line-ups - ESPN

Skip to main content Skip to navigation

play

Austria and Jordan begin their 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara, as the pair face off in a Group J encounter. With defending champions Argentina and African giants Algeria also in their group, both Austria and Jordan will look upon this encounter as a priceless chance for points.

Ralf Rangnick has led a revival of Austrian football since his appointment in 2022, and it culminated in a first World Cup appearance since 1998. They followed their Euro 2024 round-of-16 exit with 11 victories in their next 18 games, thus raising hopes of a positive World Cup campaign.

For Jordan however, a debut appearance at the World Cup is widely expected to end in a group stage exit, although coach Jamal Sellami dreams bigger. Defensive solidity was their trademark as they impressed in World Cup qualification, and Jordan will need every bit of it given their tough group in the World Cup.

Here's everything you need to know about Wednesday's game.

## How to watch:

The match will be available on BBC

Austria vs Jordan– Predicted lineup and team news

Austria kick off their long-awaited World Cup campaign against debutants Jordan at Levi’s Stadium on Wednesday morning.

Head coach Ralf Rangnick has completely revitalised the national side, instilling a high-intensity pressing identity. The Burschen easily topped their UEFA qualification group by winning six of their eight matches.

Advertisement

Advertisement

They look to secure a dominant victory to establish an early advantage in Group J. With a daunting match against group favourites Argentina on the horizon, collecting three points here is essential. Rangnick will demand immediate tactical focus to break down a defensive opposition block.

## Austria team news

Rangnick must navigate this opening match without the services of midfielder Christoph Baumgartner. The creative star suffered a severe thigh injury during pre-tournament preparations. In his absence, Marcel Sabitzer w

news_items.title: Austria vs Jordan: 2026 World Cup Group J Preview, Lineups & Predictions | Mathrubhumi English
news_items.author: Sports Desk
news_items.text:
Austria vs Jordan: 2026 World Cup Group J Preview, Lineups & Predictions | Mathrubhumi English

FIFA World Cup 2026

Last Updated: 16 June 2026, 01:12 PM IST

#### Share

Under the California sun, Austria’s high‑octane pressing game, Bundesliga backbone and attacking depth make them strong favourites, but Jordan’s organised back three and Al‑Tamari‑led counter‑punch mean this Group J opener could offer more resistance than the rankings suggest.

Austria kick off their FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign against tournament debutants Jordan in a Group J opener at Levi's Stadium, Santa Clara in the San Francisco Bay Area, with Ralf Rangnick's high‑pressing, Bundesliga‑laden side widely tipped to dominate possession against a compact Jordanian outfit built around Mousa Al‑Tamari’s counter‑attacking threat.

Under the California sun, Austria's high‑octane pressing game, Bundesliga backbone and attacking depth make them strong favourites, but Jordan's organised back three and Al‑Tamari‑led counter‑punch mean this Group J opener could offer more resistance than the rankings suggest.

#### Also Read

#### Iraq vs Norway World Cup 2026 Group I showdown in Boston: Predicted lineups, strategy and pr

# Team News: Austria vs. Jordan injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Ellis Stevens| 16 Jun 2026 05:00, Last updated: 16 Jun 2026 05:00

© Imago / STEINSIEK.CH

Austria and Jordan get their respective 2026 World Cup campaigns underway when they meet in a Group J clash on Wednesday morning.

Austria return to the World Cup stage for the first time since 1998, while Jordan will be making their first-ever appearance at the competition.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up all of the latest team news for both nations.

---

## AUSTRIA vs. JORDAN

## AUSTRIA

Out: None

Doubtful: David Alaba(unspecified), Florian Grillitsch(unspecified), Patrick Wimmer(unspecified)

# Austria vs Jordan team news and predicted lineups

- Harry Edwards
- 16 hours ago

- Copied

Austria get their World Cup 2026 campaign underway when they face Jordan on Wednesday at 5am (UK time).

Austria are back at the World Cup after a 28-year absence, since their group-stage exit in 1998. It was their longest absence from the tournament, beating the 20 years they missed out between 1958 and 1978. Austria had early success at the World Cup with strong finishes in 1934 and 1954, but managed just one win across 1990 and 1998.

Jordan, meanwhile, are one of the World Cup debutants at this summer’s tournament. They have been placed in a tough group but will be history makers whatever happens. And they’ll have already seen Curacao score in their very first World Cup.

This will be the first meeting between these two sides.

## Austria vs Jordan team news

### Austria team news

Austria and Jordan begin their 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara, as the pair face off in a Group J encounter. With defending champions Argentina and African giants Algeria also in their group, both Austria and Jordan will look upon this encounter as a priceless chance for points.

Ralf Rangnick has led a revival of Austrian football since his appointment in 2022, and it culminated in a first World Cup appearance since 1998. They followed their Euro 2024 round-of-16 exit with 11 victories in their next 18 games, thus raising hopes of a positive World Cup campaign.

For Jordan however, a debut appearance at the World Cup is widely expected to end in a group stage exit, although coach Jamal Sellami dreams bigger. Defensive solidity was their trademark as they impressed in World Cup qualification, and Jordan will need every bit of it given their tough group in the World Cup.

Here's everything you need to know about Wednesday's game.
